Idiomatic Expressions

  • 夫习防谭妖其来久矣 - Classical opening phrase meaning 'Indeed, the practice of discussing strange and marvelous things has been long established.' '夫' is a discourse particle, '习防' means 'practice and discuss,' and '谭妖' means 'talk about strange phenomena.'
  • 非博闻强识何以知之 - Rhetorical question meaning 'Without broad knowledge and strong memory, how could one know it?' '博闻' means 'broad knowledge,' and '强识' means 'strong memory.'
  • 然须抄録见知雌黄事语其虚则源流具在 - Complex phrase meaning 'However, one must record and know the true and false versions of such stories, as their origins and developments are all present.' '抄録' means 'to copy and record,' '见知' means 'to know,' '雌黄' refers to false or altered information, and '事' refers to various matters or stories.
  • 定其实则姓氏罔差 - Means 'To determine their true nature, the names and surnames are without error.' '定' means 'to determine,' '其实' means 'their true nature,' and '罔差' means 'without error.'
  • 既悟英彦之讨论亦是宾朋之节奏 - Means 'Having understood the discussions of the talented, it is also the rhythm of the guests.' '悟' means 'to understand,' '英彦' refers to talented individuals, '讨论' means 'discussion,' '宾朋' means 'guests and friends,' and '节奏' means 'rhythm.'
  • 若纂集克备即应对如流 - Means 'If compiled and completed, it will flow smoothly in response.' '纂集' means 'to compile,' '克备' means 'completed,' and '应对如流' means 'respond smoothly.'
  • 余于志西斋从宦北阙因寻往事輙议编题成一卷 - Means 'I, at my western study, serving in the northern capital, upon recalling past events, would then discuss and compile, completing it into a volume.' '志西斋' refers to the author's study, '从宦北阙' means 'serving in the northern capital,' '寻往事' means 'recalling past events,' and '编题' means 'to compile and title.'
  • 非徒但资笑语抑亦粗显箴规 - Means 'Not only to provide laughter and conversation, but also to roughly reveal admonitions.' '非徒' means 'not only,' '资笑语' means 'to provide laughter and conversation,' '抑亦' means 'but also,' and '粗显箴规' means 'roughly reveal admonitions.'
  • 或冀逆耳之辞稍获周身之诫 - Means 'Perhaps hoping that words that may be unpleasant to hear will slightly gain warnings for self-preservation.' '冀' means 'hope,' '逆耳之辞' means 'words that may be unpleasant to hear,' and '周身之诫' means 'warnings for self-preservation.'
  • 只求同已何必褾名是称谷神子 - Means 'Only seeking to align with oneself, why bother with a title? This is called 'Gushenzi.' '同已' means 'align with oneself,' '褾名' means 'title,' and '谷神子' is a self-designation or pseudonym.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 博异记 - A historical or literary work that records strange and marvelous things. The exact nature of this work is unclear from the text alone.
  • 谷神子 - A self-designation or pseudonym used by the author. '谷神' can be interpreted as 'the spirit of the valley,' symbolizing wisdom or mystery.

Proper Nouns

  • 谷神子 - The author's self-designation or pseudonym.

[2]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 博異説一卷 - Refers to a single volume of a work titled 'Bo Yi Shuo,' which is a collection of strange stories or tales.
  • 記唐初及中世事 - Means 'records events of the early and middle Tang periods.'
  • 或曰鄭還古作 - Means 'some say it was written by Zheng Huan Gu.' The phrase '或曰' indicates uncertainty or hearsay.
  • 按鼂氏云題曰谷神子纂序稱其書 - Could mean 'According to Zhao Shi, the title is 'Gu Shen Zi' and the preface claims the book...' or 'According to Zhao Shi, the title is 'Gu Shen Zi' and the preface is attributed to him.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 唐初及中世 - Refers to the early and middle periods of the Tang Dynasty, a significant era in Chinese history known for its cultural and political achievements.
  • 鄭還古 - A name that is not well-documented in historical records, indicating that the authorship is uncertain.
  • 鼂氏 - Likely refers to Zhao Shi, a scholar or historian whose works are referenced in the text.
  • 谷神子 - A pseudonym or title, possibly referring to the compiler or a character within the book.
  • 誌怪 - A genre of literature that records strange or supernatural events, often associated with folklore and mythology.

Proper Nouns

  • 博異説 - The title of the work.
  • 鄭還古 - The name of the author, if the claim is true.
  • 鼂氏 - Likely Zhao Shi, a scholar or historian.
  • 谷神子 - A pseudonym or title, possibly referring to the compiler or a character within the book.

[3]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 粉乃当时耳仲躬与坐而讯之曰 - Introduces a dialogue; translates to 'Fen was sitting with Zong Gong at the time and asked him.'
  • 卿何以杀人 - Direct question meaning 'Why did you kill someone?'; '卿' is a respectful form of address.
  • 妾实非杀人者 - '妾' is a humble term for a woman addressing a man; '实非杀人者' means 'I am not the one who killed anyone.'
  • 此井有毒龙 - Metaphorical or supernatural element; '有毒龙' means 'has a venomous dragon.'
  • 自汉朝绛侯居于兹遂穿此井 - Historical allusion; '自汉朝绛侯居于兹遂穿此井' means 'Since the Han Dynasty, Duke Jianguo (Jiang Hou) lived here and dug this well.'
  • 洛城内都有五毒龙 - Metaphorical or supernatural element; '五毒龙' means 'five venomous dragons.'
  • 每相蒙蔽 - Means 'they often conceal each other.'
  • 天命追徴多故为不赴集役 - Means 'due to many reasons, they do not respond to the imperial summons.'
  • 好食人血 - Means 'enjoys eating human blood.'
  • 自汉已来已杀三千七百人矣 - Historical account; '自汉已来已杀三千七百人矣' means 'since the Han Dynasty, it has already killed 3,700 people.'
  • 而水不曾耗涸 - Means 'but the water has never dried up.'
  • 某乃国初方堕于井 - Means 'I just fell into the well at the beginning of the kingdom.'
  • 遂为龙所驱使为妖惑以诱人用供龙所食 - Means 'so it is driven by the dragon to bewitch people and lure them to be eaten by the dragon.'
  • 其于辛苦情所非愿 - Means 'it does not wish to endure hardship.'
  • 昨为太一使者交替天下龙神尽湏集驾 - Means 'yesterday, as the messenger of Taiyi, all the dragons in the world had to gather.'
  • 昨夜子时已朝太一矣 - Means 'last night at midnight, it had already paid homage to Taiyi.'
  • 兼为河南旱被勘责三数日方回 - Means 'also, because of the drought in Henan, it was inspected and will return in three or four days.'
  • 今井内已无水 - Means 'now there is no water in the well.'
  • 君子诚能命匠淘之则获脱难矣 - Means 'if a gentleman can order a craftsman to dredge the well, he will escape the danger.'
  • 如脱难愿于君子一生奉义世间之事无所不致 - Means 'if he escapes the danger, he will serve the gentleman faithfully for life and accomplish anything in the world.'
  • 言讫便失所在 - Means 'after he finished speaking, he disappeared.'
  • 仲躬乃当时命匠令一信者与匠同入井中 - Means 'Zong Gong then ordered a craftsman and a trusted person to enter the well together.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 自汉朝绛侯居于兹遂穿此井 - Refers to the Han Dynasty and the historical figure Duke Jianguo (Jiang Hou), who is said to have dug the well.
  • 太一 - Deity in Chinese mythology, often associated with the supreme god.
  • 河南旱 - Refers to a drought in Henan, a historical event that adds context to the narrative.
  • 君子 - Term for a noble or virtuous person, often used in classical Chinese literature.

Proper Nouns

  • 粉 - Name of the woman who is being questioned.
  • 仲躬 - Name of the person questioning the woman.
  • 太一 - Deity in Chinese mythology.
  • 绛侯 - Historical figure Duke Jianguo (Jiang Hou).
  • 河南 - Region of Henan, known for its historical significance.
  • 君子 - Term for a noble or virtuous person.

[4]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 見異物即令之 - Upon seeing strange objects, immediately command them to be brought to the bottom. The character  is uncertain; it may refer to a specific action or object. For the sake of clarity, it has been interpreted as 'bring' or 'retrieve.'
  • 至底無別物 - To the bottom, there is no other thing. This phrase emphasizes the absence of anything unusual.
  • 唯獲古銅鏡一枚 - Only one ancient bronze mirror is obtained. This phrase highlights the uniqueness of the discovery.
  • 面闊七寸八分 - The face is seven and eight-tenths inches wide. This measurement is precise and should be translated accurately.
  • 仲躬令洗淨安匣中 - Zhong Gong ordered it to be washed clean and placed in a box. This describes a ritualistic or respectful treatment of the mirror.
  • 焚香以潔之 - Burn incense to purify it. This indicates a purification ritual.
  • 斯乃敬元穎者也 - This is the one who respects Yuan Ying. This phrase identifies the mirror as being associated with Yuan Ying.
  • 一更後忽見元穎自門而入 - After the first watch, Yuan Ying suddenly appeared from the door. This describes a supernatural event.
  • 直造燭前設拜 - He went straight to the candle and performed a bow. This indicates a formal greeting.
  • 謝以生成之恩 - Thanked for the kindness of being born. This phrase expresses gratitude for life.
  • 煦衣濁水泥之下 - Wearing clothes in the muddy water. This describes Yuan Ying's state.
  • 某本師曠所鑄十二鏡之第七者也 - I am the seventh of the twelve mirrors cast by Shikuang. This identifies Yuan Ying's origin.
  • 其鑄時皆以日月為大小之差 - At the time of casting, they were all made with the sun and moon as the difference in size. This explains the method of casting.
  • 元穎則七月七日午時鑄者也 - Yuan Ying was cast on the seventh day of the seventh month at noon. This provides a specific date and time.
  • 貞觀中為許敬宗婢蘭苔所墮 - During the Zhenguan era, it fell due to the servant Lan Tai of Xu Jingzong. This explains how the mirror ended up in the well.
  • 以此井水深兼毒龍氣所苦 - Because the well water is deep and troubled by the poisonous dragon's breath. This describes the well's condition.
  • 人入者悶絶而不可取 - Those who enter it will faint and cannot be retrieved. This warns of the well's danger.
  • 遂為毒龍所役 - Thus, it was enslaved by the poisonous dragon. This explains Yuan Ying's fate.
  • 幸遇君子正直者乃防重見人間爾 - Fortunately, encountering a virtuous and upright person allows one to avoid being seen again by people. This expresses gratitude for Zhong Gong's intervention.
  • 然明晨內望君子移出此宅 - However, tomorrow morning, within the house, look for the gentleman to move out of this house. This is a request or warning.
  • 仲躬曰某以用錢僦居 - Zhong Gong said, 'I rented this place with money.' This explains his situation.
  • 今移出何以取措定之所 - Now, if I move out, where will I find a fixed place to stay? This expresses his concern.
  • 元穎曰但請君子飾裝一無憂矣 - Yuan Ying said, 'Please just have the gentleman prepare his travel, and there will be no worries.' This offers a solution.
  • 言訖再拜云自此去不復見形矣 - After saying this, he bowed twice and said, 'From now on, I will not be seen again.' This marks the end of Yuan Ying's appearance.
  • 仲躬遽留之問曰汝以紅緑 - Zhong Gong hurriedly stopped him and asked, 'You with red and green.' This is an incomplete sentence, possibly indicating a question about Yuan Ying's appearance.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 仲躬 - A name, likely a fictional or historical figure in the story.
  • 元穎 - A name, likely a fictional or historical figure in the story.
  • 師曠 - A legendary musician and statesman from the Spring and Autumn period, known for his wisdom and skill in casting mirrors.
  • 貞觀 - The era name of Emperor Taizong of Tang (627–649), a period of prosperity and cultural flourishing.
  • 許敬宗 - A high-ranking official during the Tang dynasty, known for his political influence.
  • 蘭苔 - A name, likely a fictional or historical figure in the story.
  • 毒龍 - A mythical creature, often associated with danger and chaos in Chinese folklore.

Proper Nouns

  • 仲躬 - A name, likely a fictional or historical figure in the story.
  • 元穎 - A name, likely a fictional or historical figure in the story.
  • 師曠 - A legendary musician and statesman from the Spring and Autumn period.
  • 貞觀 - The era name of Emperor Taizong of Tang.
  • 許敬宗 - A high-ranking official during the Tang dynasty.
  • 蘭苔 - A name, likely a fictional or historical figure in the story.
  • 毒龍 - A mythical creature in Chinese folklore.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 脂粉之丽何以诱女子小儿也 - Rhetorical question meaning 'How does the beauty of cosmetics attract girls and boys?' Introduces the topic of the source's beauty.
  • 某变化无常 - Means 'I change without pattern' or 'I am unpredictable,' referring to the source's ability to adapt to different forms.
  • 各以所悦 - Means 'each according to what they like' or 'each in accordance with their preference.'
  • 百方谋策以供龙用 - Means 'I devise countless strategies to serve the dragon's use,' referring to the source's purpose of serving the emperor.
  • 言讫即无所见 - Means 'after speaking, I disappear,' referring to the source's ability to vanish after speaking.
  • 牙人 - Means 'broker' or 'intermediary,' referring to someone who facilitates transactions.
  • 移居 - Means 'to move' or 'to relocate,' referring to the act of moving to a new residence.
  • 夫役竝足 - Means 'laborers and servants are sufficient,' referring to the availability of labor.
  • 到斋时 - Means 'by the time of the meal' or 'when it's time to eat,' referring to the arrival of servants.
  • 立徳坊 - A place name, meaning 'Lidé Lane,' referring to a specific location.
  • 其大小价数一如清化者 - Means 'its size, price, and number are the same as those of Qinghua,' referring to the similarity between the two properties.
  • 其牙人云价直契书一无遗阙竝交割讫 - Means 'the broker said that the price, contract, and all other documents were complete and handed over,' referring to the completion of the transaction.
  • 防清化宅井无故自崩兼延及堂隍东厢一时防地 - Means 'the well of the Qinghua residence collapsed for no reason and extended to the hall and eastern wing, causing a sudden collapse,' referring to a sudden disaster.
  • 其镜背有二十八字皆科斗书以今文推而写之曰 - Means 'there are twenty-eight characters on the back of the mirror, all written in ancient script, which can be translated into modern characters as follows,' referring to the inscription on the mirror.
  • 维晋新公二年七月七日午时于首阳山前白龙潭铸成此镜 - Means 'in the second year of Jinxin, on the seventh day of the seventh month at noon, this mirror was cast at the White Dragon Pool in front of Shouyang Mountain,' referring to the date and location of the mirror's creation.

  • 女子小儿 - Refers to girls and boys, indicating the broad appeal of the source's beauty.
  • 某变化无常 - Refers to the source's ability to change form, which is a common theme in Chinese mythology and folklore.
  • 百方谋策以供龙用 - Refers to the source's purpose of serving the emperor, who is often symbolized by a dragon in Chinese culture.
  • 言讫即无所见 - Refers to the source's ability to disappear after speaking, which is a common motif in Chinese literature and mythology.
  • 牙人 - Refers to a broker or intermediary, who facilitated transactions in ancient China.
  • 移居 - Refers to the act of moving to a new residence, which was a significant event in ancient Chinese society.
  • 夫役竝足 - Refers to the availability of labor, which was essential for the construction and maintenance of residences in ancient China.
  • 到斋时 - Refers to the arrival of servants, who were responsible for preparing meals and other tasks in ancient Chinese households.
  • 立徳坊 - Refers to a specific location, which was likely a well-known area in ancient China.
  • 其大小价数一如清化者 - Refers to the similarity between the two properties, which was likely a common practice in ancient Chinese real estate transactions.
  • 其牙人云价直契书一无遗阙竝交割讫 - Refers to the completion of the transaction, which was a significant event in ancient Chinese society.
  • 防清化宅井无故自崩兼延及堂隍东厢一时防地 - Refers to a sudden disaster, which was likely a significant event in ancient Chinese society.
  • 其镜背有二十八字皆科斗书以今文推而写之曰 - Refers to the inscription on the mirror, which was likely a significant artifact in ancient Chinese society.
  • 维晋新公二年七月七日午时于首阳山前白龙潭铸成此镜 - Refers to the date and location of the mirror's creation, which was likely a significant event in ancient Chinese society.

Proper Nouns

  • 晋新公 - A title or name, likely referring to a ruler or official in ancient China.
  • 首阳山 - A place name, likely referring to a well-known mountain in ancient China.
  • 白龙潭 - A place name, likely referring to a well-known pool in ancient China.
  • 夷则之镜 - A title or name, likely referring to a specific mirror in ancient China.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 名商 - Ambiguous term, could mean 'famous merchant' or 'named merchant'.
  • 不覺 - Means 'unconsciously' or 'without realizing'.
  • 森茂 - Means 'dense and lush'.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 有少酒愿 - A phrase indicating a desire or wish, likely 'There is a little wine, I wish to...' or 'With a little wine, I wish to...'
  • 揖坐讫 - A formal expression meaning 'after bowing and sitting down,' indicating a polite greeting or seating arrangement.
  • 青衣具饮食 - 'Alone, prepare food and drink,' where '青衣' likely refers to a servant or attendant dressed in blue, preparing the meal.
  • 未吐大如斗盎 - Describes the size of the flower before it opens, meaning 'not yet bloomed, as large as a dou (a unit of measurement).'
  • 红花满树 - 'Red flowers cover the tree,' describing the appearance of the tree.
  • 执酒相揖 - 'Hold wine and bow to each other,' indicating a formal gesture of respect.
  • 萧萧泠泠 - Describes the sound of music, meaning 'rustling and clear.'
  • 杳入神仙 - 'Vanish into the realm of immortals,' suggesting a mystical or otherworldly experience.
  • 落池中 - 'Fall into the pool,' describing the action of the flowers or people falling into the water.

  • 斗盎 - A unit of measurement, indicating the size of the flower.
  • 女郎 - Refers to a young woman, often used in classical literature to describe a maiden or lady.

  • 萧萧泠泠 - Describes the sound of music, often used in classical Chinese to evoke a sense of elegance and refinement.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 有感怀一章 - A poem inspired by feelings.
  • 十载一归来 - Literally 'returning once every ten years,' implying a long absence or infrequent returns.
  • 辛苦潇湘水 - Literally 'hardship in the waters of Xiao-Xiang,' a metaphor for enduring hardships.
  • 金花之素 - Refers to a type of fine paper or silk, often used for writing or painting.
  • 银字扎之 - Means 'tied with silver letters,' indicating a decorative or ceremonial binding.
  • 白玉为管 - Refers to a writing brush made of white jade.
  • 碧玉以颇为匣 - Means 'a jade box made of lapis lazuli,' indicating a precious container for the brush and ink.
  • 银水 - Refers to silver ink, which was a luxury item.
  • 押署 - Means 'to sign or affix one's seal,' indicating a formal act of authentication.
  • 适以弊名押署 - Means 'just to sign with my humble name.'
  • 事别非君子所论 - Means 'this is a different matter and not something a gentleman would discuss.'
  • 命发拾挥霍次 - Means 'the command to send out and distribute was given, and it was done quickly and efficiently.'
  • 郎可归舟矣 - Means 'you should return to your boat now.'
  • 欣此旅泊接奉不得郑重耳 - Means 'I am pleased to meet you here, but we cannot be too formal due to the circumstances.'

  • 海门连洞庭 - Refers to the connection between the sea and Dongting Lake, a significant geographical feature in ancient China.
  • 潇湘水 - Refers to the Xiao-Xiang River, which is associated with the region of Hunan and often used in classical literature to symbolize beauty and hardship.
  • 四更已来 - Refers to the fourth watch of the night, indicating a late hour.

Proper Nouns

  • 汉阳 - The name of the person being addressed.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 開元中 - Refers to the reign of Emperor Xuanzong of Tang (713-741 AD), a period of cultural flourishing.
  • 自吳抵京國 - Means 'from Wu to the capital,' indicating a journey from the southern region to the imperial capital.
  • 舟行至馬當山 - Indicates that the journey was by boat and reached Mǎdāng Mountain.
  • 屬風便 - Means 'happened to have a favorable wind.'
  • 貴賤至此皆合謁廟以祈风水之安 - A complex sentence structure indicating that both nobles and commoners should visit the temple to pray for safe passage.
  • 昌齡不能駐 - Means 'Wang Changling could not stop.'
  • 亦先有禱神之備 - Indicates that Wang Changling had already prepared to pray to the gods.
  • 見舟人言 - Means 'upon hearing the boatman's words.'
  • 乃命使赍酒脯紙馬獻於大王 - Indicates that Wang Changling ordered an envoy to carry wine, meat, paper horses, and other offerings to the great king.
  • 兼有一量草履子上大王夫人 - Means 'and also brought a pair of straw sandals for the great king's wife.'

  • 连饮数掬 - 'To drink several *ju* (measures)' – *掬* is a unit of volume, roughly equivalent to a handful or a cup. The phrase implies drinking a large quantity.
  • 似醉而饱 - 'As if drunk and full,' a metaphorical expression for the overwhelming effect of the drink, combining intoxication and satiety.
  • 遂为门人引下山 - 'Then the disciple led him down the mountain,' where '遂' indicates a result or consequence, and '引' means to lead or guide.
  • 每至宫阙只得于门外而不许入 - 'Whenever he arrived at the palace, he could only remain outside and was not allowed to enter,' indicating a strict restriction.
  • 如是经行半日至山趾 - 'In this way, he walked for half a day until he reached the foot of the mountain,' describing a journey of considerable length.
  • 有一国城皆是金银珉玉为宫室城楼 - 'There was a city made entirely of gold, silver, jade, and precious stones for its palaces and towers,' a vivid description of a fantastical city.
  • 以玉字题云梯仙国 - 'The city was inscribed with the character '玉' (jade) and named 'Yunti Xiangguo' (Cloud Ladder Immortal Country),' indicating both the material and the name of the place.
  • 此皆诸仙初得仙者闗送此国修行七十万日 - 'These are all the newly attained immortals who are sent to this country to cultivate for 700,000 days,' a highly stylized and numerical expression of the time required for ascension.
  • 然后得至诸天或玉京蓬莱昆阆姑射 - 'Then they may reach the various heavens or the jade capital, Penglai, Kunlun, and Gu She,' referencing famous Daoist paradises.
  • 然后方得仙官职位主箓主符主印主衣飞行自在 - 'Then they may attain official positions among the immortals, in charge of records, talismans, seals, robes, and flying freely,' describing the rewards of cultivation.
  • 既是仙国何在吾国之下界 - 'If it is an immortal realm, why is it located beneath our world?' a rhetorical question indicating a comparison of realms.
  • 吾此国是下界之上仙国也 - 'Our country is the upper immortal realm above the lower world,' a clarification of the hierarchy of realms.
  • 汝国之上还有仙国如吾国亦曰梯仙国 - 'Above your country there are other immortal realms like ours, also called 'Yunti Xiangguo' (Cloud Ladder Immortal Country),' indicating a layered hierarchy of realms.
  • 异无所异 - 'There is no difference,' a paradoxical expression emphasizing similarity.
  • 言毕谓工人曰卿可归矣 - 'After finishing, he said to the worker, 'You may return now,' a formal closing to the dialogue.
  • 遂却上山聿寻来路 - 'He then returned up the mountain, seeking the way he had come,' indicating a reversal of direction.
  • 又令饮白泉数掬欲至山顶求来穴 - 'He again made him drink several *ju* of the white spring, wishing to reach the mountain top and find the entrance,' describing the continued journey and ritual.
  • 汝来此虽顷刻 - 'You came here only for a moment,' indicating the contrast between the brief time experienced and the long journey in the other realm.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 门人 - 'Disciple' or 'follower,' a common term in Daoist and Confucian traditions for a student or apprentice.
  • 宫阙 - 'Palaces and halls,' typically associated with the heavenly or imperial realm, suggesting a divine or celestial setting.
  • 云梯仙国 - 'Yunti Xiangguo' (Cloud Ladder Immortal Country), a name of a Daoist paradise. The term '云梯' (cloud ladder) may refer to a symbolic or literal means of ascending to heaven.
  • 玉京蓬莱昆阆姑射 - These are all famous Daoist paradises: '玉京' (Yujing) – The 'Jade Capital,' a Daoist heaven; '蓬莱' (Penglai) – One of the three legendary immortality islands in Daoist cosmology; '昆阆' (Kunlun) – Kunlun Mountain, a mythical mountain associated with immortals and the Queen Mother of Heaven; '姑射' (Gushè) – A Daoist paradise mentioned in the *Zhuangzi*.
  • 主箓主符主印主衣 - These are roles among immortals, each with a specific function: '主箓' – 'Master of records' (immortal records or fate); '主符' – 'Master of talismans' (magical symbols); '主印' – 'Master of seals' (authoritative seals or documents); '主衣' – 'Master of robes' (clothing or attire of immortals).
  • 飞行自在 - 'Flying freely,' a common Daoist ideal of transcendence and liberation.
  • 白泉 - 'White spring,' likely a sacred or magical spring, common in Daoist texts as a source of immortality or transformation.
  • 顷刻 - 'A moment,' a term used to express the contrast between subjective time and objective time, common in Daoist and Buddhist texts.
